incorrects.description: Student set wrong number of containers on first attempt

steps.text: Check the scenario. How many packs are on the shelf?

incorrects.description: Student set wrong number of containers on second attempt

params.description: The phrase '4 packs' highlights in the scenario text.

steps.text: The scenario says 4 packs of juice boxes. Each pack is one group. How many groups do you need?

incorrects.description: Student set wrong number of containers three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System sets the container count to 4.

steps.text: Let me show you. The scenario says 4 packs of juice boxes. Each pack is one group, so we need 4 containers. When we see packs, we count how many packs there are. That tells us how many groups.

incorrects.description: Student set wrong number of items per container on first attempt

steps.text: Look at the scenario again. How many juice boxes are in each pack?

incorrects.description: Student set wrong number of items per container on second attempt

params.description: The phrase '6 juice boxes' highlights in the scenario text.

steps.text: The scenario says each pack has 6 juice boxes. That means 6 items go in each container. How many items per container?

incorrects.description: Student set wrong number of items per container three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System sets 6 items in each container.

steps.text: Here's how it works. The scenario says each pack has 6 juice boxes. That 6 is the number that goes in every container. When we see 'each pack has 6,' that tells us the size of each group. The answer is 6 items per container because every pack has the same amount.
